what: Convert Accelerometry Data into Activity Intervals — accelerometry2Intervals
title: Convert Accelerometry Data into Activity Intervals — accelerometry2Intervals
text: Processes accelerometry data generate structured list intervals. employs iterative "dependency resolver" approach, allowing interval-defining functions reference results one another. function repeatedly attempts resolve intervals return `NULL` calculated progress can made.

title: Convert Accelerometry Data into Activity Intervals — accelerometry2Intervals
Convert Accelerometry Data into Activity Intervals — accelerometry2Intervals
text: driver Function. data loader "factory" function , called without arguments `driver()`, returns primary accelerometry data frame. defineIntervals List. list containing functions used define intervals. may include `.dependences` element, elements functions processed. function must accept arguments `df`, `intervals`, `activity_log`, `...`. activity_log Data frame. log activities used reference calculating specific time windows intervals. ... Additional arguments passed individual interval functions.

title: Convert Accelerometry Data into Activity Intervals — accelerometry2Intervals
text: list containing successfully calculated intervals. elements remained `NULL` process terminates filtered .

title: Convert Accelerometry Data into Activity Intervals — accelerometry2Intervals
text: algorithm uses `repeat` loop iterate functions `defineIntervals`. function fails returns `NULL`, system assumes dependency currently missing retry next iteration. Convergence reached : 1. intervals successfully resolved (`NULL` values remain). 2. complete pass functions results new resolved intervals (indicating circular dependency unresolvable error).

title: Convert Accelerometry Data into Activity Intervals — accelerometry2Intervals
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic usage example: results <- accelerometry2Intervals(   driver = function() my_data_loading_func(),   defineIntervals = my_interval_logic_list,   activity_log = user_log ) } # }

what: Create a summery for the column .criterio of a data frame representing accelerometer data — getSummary
title: Create a summery for the column .criterio of a data frame representing accelerometer data — getSummary
text: Generates summary epoch BIN file Aggregates accelerometer data day produce summaries wear time, non-wear time, valid days. Computes overall averages weighted statistics.

title: Create a summery for the column .criterio of a data frame representing accelerometer data — getSummary
code: getSummary(   df,   offset = dhours(0),   minimoHorasValidas = 20,   maximoHorasNonWear = 2,   durBoutMin = dseconds(5) )  getSummary(   df,   offset = dhours(0),   minimoHorasValidas = 20,   maximoHorasNonWear = 2,   durBoutMin = dseconds(5) )

title: Create a summery for the column .criterio of a data frame representing accelerometer data — getSummary
text: df data frame containing accelerometer data timestamp column, alongside logical criteria like .criterioRaw, .criterioBout, .criterioNW. offset lubridate::Duration offset added timestamps shift definition "day". Default 0. minimoHorasValidas Minimum number valid 'wear' hours required day considered valid. Default 20. maximoHorasNonWear Maximum number allowed 'non-wear' hours per day. Default 2. durBoutMin Minimum duration required compute valid intervals using criterio2Interval. Default 5 seconds.

title: Create a summery for the column .criterio of a data frame representing accelerometer data — getSummary
text: list summary. info use define activity variables daily basis global value. list containing: dailyTable (daily summary tibble), average (simple average sum), weightedaverage (weighted valid wear hours), totalValidHours, intervals.

text: df data frame representing accelerometry data timestamp column. first Logical. TRUE, includes first boundary interval. Default TRUE. last Logical. TRUE, includes last boundary interval. Default TRUE. offsetLabels offset added labels (names) generated intervals. Default 0 hours. starts duration shift applied start times. Default 0 hours. duration duration generated interval block. title: Generate Daily Named Intervals — measureInterval_24h
text: df data frame representing accelerometry data timestamp column. first Logical. TRUE, includes first boundary interval. Default TRUE. last Logical. TRUE, includes last boundary interval. Default TRUE. offsetLabels offset added daily labels (names). Default 0 hours. starts duration offset applied start times. Default 0 hours. duration duration generated interval block. Default 1 day (period(1, "days")). .isOn Optional explicit interval bounds delimit sequence generation. withName Logical. TRUE, assigns formatted date string names interval. Default TRUE.

what: Search Bouts by Dynamic Criteria — searchByCriteria
title: Search Bouts by Dynamic Criteria — searchByCriteria
text: Dynamically applies specific criterion string dataframe subsequently groups consecutive positively evaluated epochs distinct intervals (bouts) based minimum percentage duration thresholds.

title: Search Bouts by Dynamic Criteria — searchByCriteria
code: searchByCriteria(   df,   EXPRESSION,   pctBouts = 0.8,   durBoutMin = dminutes(1),   runmean = FALSE,   useNW = TRUE )

title: Search Bouts by Dynamic Criteria — searchByCriteria
text: df data frame timeline/epoch measurements. EXPRESSION logical expression string (e.g., `"ENMO > 0.05"`) dynamically parsed. pctBouts required fraction (percentage 0 1) time within window criteria must hold. Default 0.8. durBoutMin required minimum continuous rolling duration bout confirmed. Default 1 minute. runmean Logical. TRUE, applies running mean smoothing filter globally referenced fields searching. Default FALSE. useNW Logical. TRUE, avoids returning bouts detected NonWear time. Default TRUE.

title: Search Bouts by Dynamic Criteria — searchByCriteria
text: list/vector validated interval bouts (start end times).

title: Find Touched Intervals — touchedIntervals
text: Identifies intervals what_n overlap touch intervals when_n. Unlike intersection, returns original untouched bounds matching segments.

title: Find Touched Intervals — touchedIntervals

title: Find Touched Intervals — touchedIntervals
text: what_n primary interval vector list. when_n target interval vector list overlaps checked. useNames Logical. TRUE, preserves/applies names. short Logical. TRUE, removes NULL (non-touching) intervals result. operator custom function/operator apply touching intervals. Defaults (identity).

title: Find Touched Intervals — touchedIntervals
text: consolidated vector/list original intervals what_n overlapped when_n.
